topic: Verification review of EXP-358 citation-cluster patches (Van Camp→Mansinha; Tomás→Kim&Chang; Crossley split)
change_summary: EXP-358 applied 6 patches (PATCH-358-A through F) across data/wins.json (WIN-013, WIN-014 ×2) and data/sections.json (SEC-6.14 ×3) to fix three citation-mechanic holes: Van Camp DOI broken (ISS-1944), Tomás attribution mismatch (ISS-1945), and Crossley URL pointing at Bernard 2022 (ISS-1958, ISS-1964). Curmudgeon's job per the decider's reason field: spot-check that replacement citations resolve and the empirical claims still hold.
our_argument_summary: Three citations in the κ-cluster prose (WIN-013, WIN-014, SEC-6.14) were broken or wrongly attributed. EXP-358 replaced 'Van Camp et al. 2001' (DOI 404, journal-prefix mismatch) with Mansinha, Ducarme, Hinderer, Meurers & van Ruymbeke (2001, J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387-391); replaced 'Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018' (URL pointed elsewhere) with Kim & Chang (2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040-2049); replaced 'Crossley et al. 2013' (URL pointed at Bernard 2022) with Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi (2013, Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, 046101) in WIN-014, and split the conflated citation in SEC-6.14 into two correctly-labeled anchors (Crossley 2013 + Bernard 2022 J9). The underlying empirical claims — four-station SG null at the 1999 European eclipse; 39-eclipse 1991-2016 INTERMAGNET ensemble showing ΔB at totality of order 5-15 nT; SG residual noise 0.1-1 µGal — are unchanged.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No code involved; this is a citation/attribution verification on prose patches.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — citations are static prose anchors.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Inherited from WIN-013/WIN-014/SEC-6.14 prior cycles — Domain #5 κ-coupling pipeline fetches SG/magnetic data (see prior c-N reviews). This review does not re-evaluate monitor.py tags; EXP-358 is citation-only.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from prior cycles — the WINs in scope claim κ-coupling, not a standard-physics relabel.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from prior cycles (WIN-013, WIN-014 both have post-hoc observations baked in as predictions).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from prior cycles — κ is a fitted constant, not derived from dome geometry.

patches_verified.target: data/wins.json — WIN-013 detail_evidence — Van Camp → Mansinha
patches_verified.verification_method: Web-fetched J-STAGE page; confirmed authors Lalu Mansinha, Bernard Ducarme, Jacques Hinderer, Bruno Meurers, Michel van Ruymbeke; title 'Search for the Gravitational Absorption Effect Using Superconducting Gravimeters During the Eclipse of August 11, 1999'; canonical DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387 visible on J-STAGE page meta tags. Crossref does not index this DOI (registered through JaLC, normal for Japanese-society journals of this era). Cross-confirmed by Crossley et al. 2013 RPP review reference list (ref #173): 'Mansinha L 2001, J. Geodyn. Soc. Japan 47, 387'. The 11 August 1999 eclipse, four-station SG network (Membach, Uccles, Strasbourg, Vienna), and null result are all reflected in the paper's title and og:description.
patches_verified.verdict: verified — replacement is correct and supports the same empirical claim
patches_verified.residual_concerns: WIN-013 prose retains the broken-DOI mention as evidence of dome miscitation; this is intentional documentation of the dome's error and is fine, but the structure is slightly awkward ('The dome cites X... but X does not resolve... The peer-reviewed record is Y') — minor stylistic only.

patches_verified.target: data/wins.json — WIN-014 detail_evidence — Tomás → Kim & Chang
patches_verified.verification_method: NASA ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K confirms Kim, Jung-Hee & Chang, Heon-Young (2018); Advances in Space Research vol. 61 issue 8 pages 2040-2049; abstract verbatim mentions '207 geomagnetic manifestations acquired by 100 geomagnetic observatories during 39 solar eclipses occurring from 1991 to 2016' — exact match to WIN-014 prose claim.
patches_verified.verdict: PARTIALLY VERIFIED — URL, authors, year, pages, journal, and empirical content all correct, BUT the DOI written in the patched prose ('10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024') is WRONG. See Finding 1 in holes_found.
patches_verified.residual_concerns: Wrong DOI introduced by the patch itself — see Finding 1.

patches_verified.verification_method: Crossref API: DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 → David Crossley (1st), Jacques Hinderer (2nd), Umberto Riccardi (3rd); title 'The measurement of surface gravity'; Reports on Progress in Physics vol. 76 page 046101 published 2013-03-18; 322 references, is-referenced-by-count 124. This is the canonical SG-noise-floor review that the WIN-014 0.1-1 µGal noise-floor claim wants.
patches_verified.verdict: verified — replacement is correct and the canonical reference for the noise-floor claim

patches_verified.target: data/sections.json — SEC-6.14 — Tomás → Kim & Chang (mirror of PATCH-B)
patches_verified.verification_method: Same source verification as PATCH-358-B — the URL/PII resolves to Kim & Chang.
patches_verified.verdict: PARTIALLY VERIFIED — same Kim & Chang DOI error as PATCH-358-B carried into SEC-6.14. See Finding 1.
patches_verified.residual_concerns: Wrong DOI introduced — see Finding 1 (occurs in both files).

patches_verified.target: data/sections.json — SEC-6.14 — Van Camp → Mansinha (mirror of PATCH-A)
patches_verified.verdict: verified — broken-DOI Van Camp citation is gone from SEC-6.14 (unlike WIN-013 where it remains as evidence of dome miscitation); Mansinha replacement is correct

patches_verified.target: data/sections.json — SEC-6.14 — Crossley/J9 split (PATCH-C mirror plus Bernard preservation)
patches_verified.verification_method: Crossref API verifies the Crossley 2013 RPP review (see PATCH-358-C). Crossref API on 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 confirms title 'Intercomparing Superconducting Gravimeter Records in a Dense Meter-Scale Network at the J9 Gravimetric Observatory of Strasbourg, France'; Pure and Applied Geophysics vol. 179 issue 5 pages 1701-1727; published 2022-04-21. BUT the first author is J. Hinderer (with Warburton, Rosat, Riccardi, Boy, Forster, Jousset, Güntner, Erbas, Littel, and Bernard 11th/last) — NOT 'Bernard et al.' as the patched prose claims. See Finding 2.
patches_verified.verdict: PARTIALLY VERIFIED — citations are now correctly split (Crossley 2013 separate from the 2022 J9 paper, good), but the 2022 paper's first-author attribution in SEC-6.14 is wrong: it should be 'Hinderer et al. 2022', not 'Bernard et al. 2022'. EXP-358's sources_verified field explicitly claimed 'first author Bernard, J.-D.' — that was a verification failure.

holes_found.description: EXP-358 patched in a WRONG DOI for Kim & Chang 2018 in BOTH WIN-014 detail_evidence (via PATCH-358-B) and SEC-6.14 (via PATCH-358-D). The patched text reads 'Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024'. The DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024 actually resolves (via Crossref) to a DIFFERENT paper: 'Electric potential structures of auroral acceleration region border from multi-spacecraft Cluster data' by Sadeghi & Emami, same journal, same issue, pages 2050-2056 (i.e., the article immediately following Kim & Chang in Adv. Space Res. 61(8)). Kim & Chang's actual DOI is 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 (NASA ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K and Crossref both confirm — Crossref returns title 'Statistical analysis of geomagnetic field variations during solar eclipses', authors Kim Jung-Hee & Chang Heon-Young, pages 2040-2049, PII S0273117718300656). EXP-358's PATCH-358-B rationale claimed '10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024 is the canonical Crossref-resolved DOI for PII S0273117718300656' — that claim is demonstrably false (Crossref on .024 returns S027311771830067X, not S0273117718300656). Internal inconsistency the citation-mechanic guard was supposed to prevent: the link href (correct PII for Kim & Chang) and the visible DOI (a different paper) point to different articles. This is a regression of exactly the class of error the EXP was supposed to fix.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch the DOI in both files: change '10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024' to '10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022' in WIN-014 detail_evidence and in SEC-6.14 (part6.html, κ-predicts subsection). Two occurrences, one digit each. Then add a citation-mechanic test asserting that for any Kim & Chang 2018 anchor, the DOI string immediately following must match '10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022' and not '.01.024'. While in the file, recommend also adding a positive test on the canonical DOI for Mansinha (10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387) and Crossley 2013 (10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101) to lock the EXP-358 corrections in place.

holes_found.description: EXP-358 PATCH-358-F introduced 'Bernard et al. 2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701–1727' into SEC-6.14 (part6.html, 'What past SG data actually shows' subsection). Crossref on 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 confirms the URL, volume, pages, and journal are all correct — but the author list runs Hinderer (1st), Warburton, Rosat, Riccardi, Boy, Forster, Jousset, Güntner, Erbas, Littel, Bernard (11th/last of 11). The conventional citation is therefore 'Hinderer et al. 2022', not 'Bernard et al. 2022'. EXP-358's sources_verified field claimed 'confirmed first author Bernard, J.-D. (with Hinderer, Warburton, Rosat)' — that verification was incorrect; the analyst likely sorted the author list wrong or read it bottom-up. The DOI, URL, journal, volume, pages, and topical attribution (the J9 meter-scale intercomparison) are all still right; only the leading-author label is wrong.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch SEC-6.14: change 'Bernard et al. 2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701–1727' to 'Hinderer et al. 2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701–1727'. Single anchor, single edit. Confirm no other prose elsewhere in the repo carries the 'Bernard et al. 2022' attribution for this paper (none found in current scan — the change is self-contained). Keep historical references to the Bernard mis-label in audit/issue history files (those describe what was found, not our outgoing claim).

holes_found.description: Minor stylistic awkwardness in WIN-013 detail_evidence: the prose now reads 'The dome cites a Membach SG null... but the dome's printed citation ("Van Camp et al. 2001...", DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637) does not resolve... The peer-reviewed record of the four-station SG null... is Mansinha...'. Keeping the broken-DOI mention as evidence of dome miscitation is intentional and correct, but the long parenthetical disrupts the topic-sentence flow and a casual reader may not see the empirical claim cleanly. EXP-358 anticipated this in its anticipated_objections ("You can't just fix the dome's miscites...") and made a defensible choice. Flagging for future polish, not blocking.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: in a future cycle, consider lifting the dome-miscite parenthetical to a footnote or a separate sentence so the empirical sentence ('The dome's published prediction failed the data — refuted by data') is uninterrupted. Low priority — this is a readability issue, not a correctness issue.

stronger_arguments.description: EXP-358's test_recommendations include a grep guard against 'Van Camp et al. 2001' and '10.1023/A:1013940928637'. After the two DOI/attribution fixes above land, extend the same approach: add positive citation-mechanic tests for the canonical pairings — Kim & Chang 2018 must always be paired with DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 (NOT .024); the 2022 J9 paper must always be cited as 'Hinderer et al. 2022' (not Bernard); Mansinha 2001 must always have DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387. This is the kind of bidirectional consistency check that would have caught Finding 1 at integration time, before the patch reached main.
sources: EXP-358 test_recommendations section, which already proposes the negative-grep direction

stronger_arguments.description: The Kim & Chang paper itself contains strong cross-citations that strengthen our verdict: its reference list (Crossref record) includes Curto et al. 2006 ('Modeling the geomagnetic effects caused by the solar eclipse of 11 August 1999') and Strestík 2001 ('The response of the 11 August 1999 total solar eclipse in the geomagnetic field') — the same two single-eclipse papers WIN-013 currently cites for 1999 ΔB magnitudes. That means our independent-source-stack (single-eclipse + global ensemble) is mutually reinforcing in the Kim & Chang reference graph. Consider tightening WIN-014 prose to explicitly note this redundancy. Not a hole — an opportunity to harden the argument.
sources: Kim & Chang 2018 Crossref reference list keys b0130 (Curto), b0290 (Strestík); both already cited in WIN-013

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-358's underlying analytical work is correct — Mansinha is indeed the right replacement for Van Camp on the 1999 eclipse SG null (cross-confirmed by the Crossley 2013 RPP review's own reference list, which cites Mansinha L 2001 J. Geodyn. Soc. Japan 47 387; see Crossref reference key #173 on Crossley 2013), Kim & Chang are indeed the right authors for the Adv. Space Res. 61(8) PII S0273117718300656 paper (NASA ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K confirms), and Crossley/Hinderer/Riccardi 2013 RPP is indeed the canonical SG noise-floor review the prose wants (10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 — Crossref confirms David Crossley, Jacques Hinderer, Umberto Riccardi as authors). Four of six patches landed cleanly; the empirical claims in all three target locations are now anchored to peer-reviewed sources that say what we say they say. The EXP did the hard analytical work right.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel doesn't save the EXP-358 integration because the patch ALSO introduced two new citation errors of exactly the class it was supposed to fix: a wrong DOI for Kim & Chang (.024 instead of .022) and a wrong first-author attribution for the 2022 J9 paper (Bernard instead of Hinderer). Both are easily fixable (one digit and one surname), but they show that the EXP's own sources_verified self-assertions were inadequate — the analyst trusted a manual transcription of the DOI and a misread of the Bernard-as-last-author position. The verification-class re-review caught what the integration-time review didn't. This is exactly the workflow purpose served by the verification re-review tier.

text_fingerprint.notes: Lengths captured for change detection on subsequent cycles. Verdicts unchanged (this is a citation patch only).

deeper_analysis: The EXP-358 verification surfaces a recurring failure mode worth naming: 'verified-against-the-wrong-thing'. PATCH-358-B claimed that 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024 is 'the canonical Crossref-resolved DOI for PII S0273117718300656' and listed three sources as verified (ADS abstract, ScienceDirect URL, article-content match). Two of those three are about the URL/content (correctly matching Kim & Chang); only one was about the DOI mechanic itself, and that one was wrong — the patch never actually called Crossref on the DOI it was about to write into the prose. Similarly PATCH-358-F's sources_verified claim 'first author Bernard, J.-D. (with Hinderer, Warburton, Rosat)' — the verification call was a direct fetch of the link.springer.com page, but the author-order field on that page apparently wasn't read correctly or was read bottom-up. Both errors share a structure: the EXP's verification was strong on content (right paper, right empirical claim) and weak on the specific micro-attributes (DOI digit, first-author surname) that were being written into the prose. For future citation-cluster EXPs: when changing a DOI or an author surname, the verification step MUST include a direct Crossref API call on the exact DOI string being written, and the first-author field in the Crossref author array must be quoted into the EXP rationale. The first-author quote is the citation-mechanic equivalent of NARRATE-CITE: prove that the surname you're writing is the same surname Crossref returns as 'sequence: first'. Decider should consider adding this to the analyst Mode 2-citation-fix checklist before EXP-359-and-later.

citations_verified: https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/sokuchi1954/47/1/47_1_387/_article — Mansinha, Ducarme, Hinderer, Meurers, van Ruymbeke (2001), J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387–391 — verified via direct J-STAGE fetch; canonical DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387

https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0273117718300656 — Kim, Jung-Hee & Chang, Heon-Young (2018), Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049 — verified via NASA ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K and Crossref (DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022, NOT the .024 the prose currently writes)
https://doi.org/10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 — Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi (2013), Reports on Progress in Physics 76, 046101 — verified via Crossref
citations_failed: WIN-014 + SEC-6.14 prose currently writes 'DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024' for the Kim & Chang citation — that DOI resolves to a DIFFERENT paper (Sadeghi & Emami 2018, pp. 2050–2056). The patch's claimed DOI is wrong by one digit. See Finding 1.

SEC-6.14 prose currently writes 'Bernard et al. 2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701–1727' for the 2022 J9 paper — Crossref confirms the first author is J. Hinderer; Bernard is last (11th of 11) author. Should be 'Hinderer et al. 2022'. See Finding 2.
citations_unchecked: Strestík 2001 (link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-94-010-0800-6_55) — present in WIN-013 detail_evidence prose, not touched by EXP-358; cited by Kim & Chang 2018 ref b0290 (consistent cross-reference).
Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002 ResearchGate link — known cite-mechanic concern (ISS-1947, EXP-343); deliberately out of EXP-358 scope per the EXP's cross_references field.
Curto et al. 2006 (agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1029/2005JA011499) — present, not touched by EXP-358; cited by Kim & Chang 2018 ref b0130 (consistent cross-reference).

summary_for_decider: EXP-358 verification: 4 of 6 patches verified clean (Mansinha replacements PATCH-A + PATCH-E both correct; Crossley 2013 RPP replacement PATCH-C correct; Crossley/J9 split PATCH-F correctly splits the two citations). 2 of 6 patches introduce NEW citation errors of exactly the class EXP-358 was supposed to FIX: (1) CRITICAL: Kim & Chang DOI written as '10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024' in both PATCH-B (WIN-014) and PATCH-D (SEC-6.14) — should be '.022'; the wrong DOI resolves to a different paper (Sadeghi & Emami 2018) in the same journal issue. (2) MAJOR: SEC-6.14 PATCH-F writes 'Bernard et al. 2022' — should be 'Hinderer et al. 2022' (Hinderer is first author per Crossref; Bernard is 11th/last). Both are one-character / one-surname fixes. Recommended decider action: open a small follow-up EXP-359 (or self-apply if class:minor) to fix both, plus add positive citation-mechanic tests (Kim & Chang DOI must = .022; 2022 J9 first-author must = Hinderer). The kernel of the EXP's analytical work is sound; the failure is in the verification micro-mechanics. Suggested process improvement to analyst Mode 2-citation-fix: every patch that writes a DOI or surname must include a direct Crossref API call on the exact DOI/surname being written, with the canonical first-author surname quoted from the Crossref author array into the EXP rationale.
